columns.name: customer_id
columns.summary: Unique customer identifier with one value per row and no analytical signal beyond lookup.

columns.name: signup_date
columns.summary: Signup timestamp spread across many dates, useful for cohort or tenure context.

columns.name: plan_tier
columns.summary: Subscription tier with a small set of values such as basic, pro, and enterprise.

columns.name: monthly_spend
columns.summary: Customer spend is heavily right-skewed, with a few very large accounts among mostly smaller ones.

columns.name: tenure_months
columns.summary: Tenure ranges from new to long-lived customers and appears broadly distributed.

columns.name: support_tickets_30d
columns.summary: Recent support volume is usually zero but includes a few extreme values, suggesting rare escalation cases.

columns.name: last_login_days
columns.summary: Days since last login varies widely, with many active users and a smaller set of inactive ones.

columns.name: region
columns.summary: Geographic market bucketed into a handful of regions.

columns.name: churned
columns.summary: Binary outcome indicating whether the customer churned, with the minority class being churned.

plan.title: Churn by Segment
plan.hypothesis: Churn is not evenly distributed and is higher in specific plan tiers or regions than in the overall sample.

plan.title: Tenure and Recency
plan.hypothesis: Customers with shorter tenure or more recent inactivity are more likely to churn than long-tenured, active customers.
